channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Rethabile Khumalo Reflects on Healing and Honours Late Mother in New Album
item.description: South African singer Rethabile Khumalo has opened up about her journey through grief and healing following the passing of her mother, Winnie Khumalo, on 7 January 2025. Her third studio album, 'Kweliz...

item.title: Stephen Colbert’s Late Show concludes amid Trump’s cryptic remarks
item.description: Stephen Colbert’s tenure as host of CBS’s Late Show comes to an end this Thursday after 33 years of the franchise, originally launched by David Letterman in 1993. Colbert took over in 2015 and has sin...

item.title: University of Venda Aligns Internal Audit Function with Global Standards

item.title: Presidential Peace Adviser Affirms Non-Interference in BARMM Political Transition

item.title: Environmental and Resource Challenges of Expanding Data Centres in India
item.description: India is rapidly becoming a hub for artificial intelligence investments, with major companies like Google and Reliance Industries planning large data centre projects in Visakhapatnam, Andhra Pradesh. ...

item.title: Tanzania PM urges partners to focus on Africa’s infrastructure and agricultural growth

item.title: A Retrospective Look at Jean Simmons’ Career and Iconic 1950s Portraits
item.description: Jean Simmons (1929–2010) was a celebrated British actress and singer whose career spanned from post-World War II British cinema to major Hollywood productions. Unlike many 1950s starlets, she was know...

item.title: NSW Premier Minns warns bracket creep is hurting workers amid high taxes
item.description: NSW Premier Chris Minns has criticised the federal Albanese Government for allowing bracket creep to affect Australian workers, arguing that wage gains for nurses, paramedics, and teachers are being o...

item.title: Practical Landscaping Tips to Boost Your Home’s Sale Value
item.description: A well-maintained garden can significantly influence a buyer's first impression of a home and even impact its perceived value. Sarah and Fiona Carver of Sage Real Estate in Toronto highlight that outd...

item.title: Economic Struggles Push Young Kenyans to Join Foreign Conflicts
item.description: High youth unemployment in Kenya is forcing many young people to take dangerous opportunities abroad, including being recruited to fight in Russia's war against Ukraine. The government has announced a...

item.description: Njombe Region dey prepare for big economic changes as major iron ore mining projects dey move forward, mainly for Ludewa District. Di National Development Corporation (NDC) dey work with foreign inves...

item.description: Africa is at a critical economic junction, with a lot of talent, innovation, and market opportunities sprouting across the continent. Yet, many African enterprises struggle to grow from small ventures...

item.description: The case against former SAPS special task force member Matipandile Sotheni, accused of murdering Witness D, centres on WhatsApp messages, phone records, and vehicle tracking data. Investigating office...

item.title: Paul McCartney required to adjust garden plans following council and neighbour feedback
item.description: Paul McCartney has been asked to modify his garden plans at his St John’s Wood residence in London following concerns raised by a neighbour and the local council. The musician initially applied to rem...

item.title: DR Congo cancels World Cup farewell events amid Ebola outbreak
item.description: The Democratic Republic of Congo (DRC) has cancelled its three-day World Cup preparation training camp and a farewell event for fans in Kinshasa due to an ongoing Ebola outbreak in the eastern regions...
